topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a digital slap across my face. My breath hitched, a sharp, ragged sound in the quiet of my bedroom. It was Professor Albright. Not a polite mention, not a shared class resource, but a post—a photo of me from the school fair, captioned with a comment that felt like a direct challenge. My hands clenched into fists on the desk, the knuckles turning white. I slammed my laptop shut so hard the screen rattled. A hot flush spread from my neck up to my cheeks. I stood up, pacing the small room, the floorboards protesting under my frantic steps. Every word he’d ever said in lecture hall suddenly tasted like ash in my mouth. I needed to find him. I needed to confront him, to demand an explanation for this invasion, for this sheer, unwarranted intrusion into my private life.

[story 2]

I stared at the phone screen, my jaw tight enough to crack. The image was undeniable: Professor Albright’s profile, my name tagged in the bio of a recent update. A slow, building pressure tightened in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. My fingers hovered over the keyboard, trembling slightly before I typed a response—a carefully constructed barrage of accusations and incredulity. The words spilled out, sharp and accusatory, each sentence laced with a simmering fury I couldn't quite contain. I reread the message three times, my eyes burning. The silence in my apartment was deafening, broken only by the frantic drumming of my pulse. I felt a deep, visceral need to dismantle this situation, to make him understand the sheer audacity of his behavior.

[story 3]

When I saw the profile picture—that familiar, overly cheerful smile—a cold, hard knot formed in my stomach. It wasn't just seeing him; it was the realization of how completely exposed I felt. My entire body tensed. I walked over to the window, staring out at the indifferent city lights, my knuckles white where I gripped the sill. A low growl started deep in my throat, a sound I immediately tried to swallow back down, but it clawed its way up. I felt a surge of pure, unadulterated indignation. This wasn't just unprofessional; it felt like a deliberate mockery. I spun around, pacing the length of my hallway, my movements jerky and sharp, driven by an overwhelming desire to make this whole ridiculous situation disappear.
